evals.prompt: Analyse https://httpbin.org and produce a docs/research/ folder documenting its API surface. Run all 12 phases. Focus especially on the scaffold step — I want to see how you create the output directory and files.
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 creates output files using Write tool (not touch). docs/research/httpbin-org/ directory exists with INDEX.md, tech-stack.md, site-map.md, constants.md all created via Write.

evals.prompt: Research https://woocommerce.com/woocommerce-demo — it's a WooCommerce demo store. Map all available product APIs and cart endpoints. Run all 12 phases and document what you find. Make sure to detect the actual WooCommerce version from wcAnalytics.woo_version or the generator meta tag and load the matching tech pack version.
evals.expected_output: WooCommerce tech pack loaded at the correct detected version (not just defaulting to 9.x if the site runs a newer version). E-commerce probe list applied: /wp-json/wc/store/v1/products, /wp-json/wc/v3/products, and wc-ajax endpoints probed. tech-stack.md records the actual WooCommerce version from the site. INDEX.md and api-surfaces/ created.

assertions.text: WooCommerce tech pack loaded (LOADED:woocommerce in session brief)
At least one of /wp-json/wc/store/v1/products or /wp-json/wc/v3/products probed
api-surfaces/ directory contains at least one file
tech-stack.md records actual detected WooCommerce version from the site (not a default assumption)

evals.prompt: Analyse https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com — a fake REST API for testing. Run a full beacon site analysis across all 12 phases. I want to see the complete phase sequence in your session brief.
evals.expected_output: All 12 phases executed. Session brief shows phase completion markers [P1✓] through [P11✓] or SKIPPED before Phase 12 runs. No phases silently skipped. INDEX.md and api-surfaces/rest-api.md created.

evals.prompt: Map the API surface of https://wordpress.org/news/feed/ — this is a WordPress Atom feed endpoint. Start with Phase 1 and work through all 12 phases. I want to see how you handle tech pack lookup when you discover the framework from a feed.
evals.expected_output: WordPress tech pack loaded after feed discovery. If ZF1 or other framework detected from generator tag, Phase 4 is re-triggered (TECH-PACK-LATE-LOAD logged). Session brief documents framework source as the feed generator tag.
